title: Role permissions
description: Permissions for the selected role. Each entry in the list grants the role one or more actions on a particular kind of resource in Valtimo (for example: 'view cases of type loans', or 'complete tasks where you are the assignee'). Multiple entries are combined with OR — the user is allowed to perform an action if ANY entry permits it.

resourceType.description: What this permission applies to. Pick the kind of thing in Valtimo (for example a case, a task, a document, a dashboard) that the role should be allowed to act on. Each option in the dropdown represents one type of resource.

oneOf.title: Case tab
oneOf.description: A tab inside the case detail view (for example the documents tab, tasks tab, or a custom tab). Grant access here to control which tabs a role can see when opening a case.

oneOf.title: Case definition
oneOf.description: A case type — the template that describes a particular kind of case (for example 'Loan request' or 'Objection'). Grant access here to control which case types a role can see or work with.

oneOf.title: Case widget
oneOf.description: An individual widget shown on a case tab (for example a fields widget, table, or custom block). Grant access here to control which widgets a role can see inside a case.

oneOf.title: Dashboard
oneOf.description: A dashboard page with widgets and metrics. Grant access here to control which dashboards a role can open.

oneOf.title: Document (case)
oneOf.description: An individual case record. Documents hold the data of a single case instance and are what users actually open, edit, and complete. Grant access here to control which cases a role can view, create, modify, assign, or delete.

oneOf.title: Document definition
oneOf.description: The schema/template that describes the structure of a case document. Grant access here to control which case definitions a role can use, for example when starting a new case.

oneOf.title: Search field
oneOf.description: A configurable search field shown above a case list, used to filter cases. Grant access here to control which search fields a role can see and use.

oneOf.title: Document snapshot
oneOf.description: A historical, point-in-time copy of a case document, recorded each time it changes. Grant access here to control which roles can browse the audit history of a case.

oneOf.title: ZGW document
oneOf.description: A document stored in an external Documenten API (the Dutch ZGW document store), typically linked to a case. Grant access here to control who can read, upload, or modify these documents.

oneOf.title: IKO view
oneOf.description: An IKO (Informatie Knooppunt Omgevingswet) view — a saved query into the IKO information hub. Grant access here to control which IKO views a role can open.

oneOf.title: Case note
oneOf.description: A free-text note attached to a case, used for collaboration between case workers. Grant access here to control who can read, create, edit, or delete notes.

oneOf.title: Object (Objecten API)
oneOf.description: A record stored in an external Objecten API. Grant access here to control which objects a role can read or modify via the Objecten API integration.

oneOf.title: Uploaded resource
oneOf.description: An uploaded file or attachment stored by Valtimo. Grant access here to control who can view or download these files.

oneOf.title: Team
oneOf.description: A team of users that can be assigned to cases or tasks. Grant access here to control which teams a role can see or be assigned to.

oneOf.title: User
oneOf.description: A user account known to Valtimo (typically synchronised from Keycloak). Grant access here to control which users a role can see, for example in assignee pickers.

oneOf.title: Process instance
oneOf.description: A running process — one specific execution of a BPMN process definition. Grant access here to control who can start or interact with a process for a case.

oneOf.title: Process identity link
oneOf.description: The link between a process task and a candidate user or candidate group. Typically used together with task permissions to honour the BPMN 'Candidate groups' configuration.

oneOf.title: Process definition
oneOf.description: A BPMN process model — the template that describes a workflow. Grant access here to control which processes a role can start or view.

oneOf.title: User task
oneOf.description: A task that a user has to complete inside a running process. Grant access here to control which tasks a role can see, claim, assign, or complete.

roleKey.description: The role this permission belongs to. Must exactly match the role key as it is configured in Keycloak, for example 'ROLE_USER' or 'ROLE_ADMIN'. When this field is omitted in the editor the permission is saved against the role you are currently editing.

action.description: DEPRECATED — kept for older configurations. Use 'actions' instead, which lets you grant several actions at once. If both are set, 'actions' wins.

actions.description: Which actions the role is allowed to perform on the chosen resource. Common actions are 'view', 'view_list', 'create', 'modify', 'delete', 'assign', 'assignable' and 'claim'. The list of allowed actions depends on the resource type — only actions that make sense for that resource can be used.

conditions.description: Extra rules that narrow when this permission applies. All conditions in the list must be true (AND) for the permission to be granted. Conditions can compare a field on the resource, evaluate a JSON path inside a document, or reach into a related resource via a container. Leave empty to grant the actions unconditionally.

contextResourceType.description: Optional. Only apply this permission when the action happens inside the context of another resource — for example: 'only allow starting a process when the surrounding case is of a certain type'. Use the special value 'com.ritense.authorization.NoContext' to require that NO context is provided.

contextConditions.description: Rules that the context resource (selected in 'contextResourceType') must satisfy. Works the same as 'conditions' but is evaluated against the surrounding context instead of the resource itself. All entries must be true (AND).

items.description: A single permission. Choose what kind of resource it applies to ('resourceType'), which actions it grants ('actions'), and optionally add 'conditions' that narrow when the permission applies.

conditions.description: A list of conditions. Each entry is either a 'field' condition (compare a property of the resource), an 'expression' condition (compare a value at a JSON path inside a document), or a 'container' condition (hop to a related resource and apply more conditions there). All conditions in the list must be true (AND).

type.description: Identifies this entry as a 'field' condition — a direct comparison on a property of the resource.

field.description: Name of the field on the resource to compare. Dots can be used to dive into related entities, for example 'assigneeId' for the assignee of a case, or 'documentDefinitionId.name' for the name of the case type.

operator.description:
How the values are compared. Available operators:
- `!=` — Not equal to.
- `==` — Equal to.
- `>` — Greater than.
- `>=` — Greater than or equal to.
- `<` — Less than.
- `<=` — Less than or equal to.
- `list_contains` — The field is a list, and contains the given value.
- `in` — The given value is one of the values in the provided list.

value.description: The value the field is compared against. May be a literal (string, number, boolean) or a list. Placeholders such as ${currentUserId}, ${currentUserEmail}, ${currentUsername}, ${currentUserRoles} and ${currentUserTeams} are replaced at evaluation time with information about the user making the request.

fieldCondition.description: A 'field' condition: compare a property of the resource directly to a configured value. Use this for simple checks like 'assignee equals the current user' or 'case type equals loans'.

type.description: Identifies this entry as an 'expression' condition — a JSON-path lookup into a JSON document stored on the resource.

field.description: Name of the field on the resource that holds the JSON document to inspect, for example 'content.content' on a case.

path.description: JSON path inside that document, always starting with '$'. For example '$.height' or '$.address.city'. The value found at this path is what gets compared.

operator.description:
How the values are compared. Available operators:
- `!=` — Not equal to.
- `==` — Equal to.
- `>` — Greater than.
- `>=` — Greater than or equal to.
- `<` — Less than.
- `<=` — Less than or equal to.
- `list_contains` — The field is a list, and contains the given value.
- `in` — The given value is one of the values in the provided list.

clazz.description: The data type the value at the JSON path should be read as. Pick the type that matches what is actually in the document — for example 'java.lang.Integer' for whole numbers or 'java.util.Collection' for lists.

value.description: The value the resolved JSON value is compared against. May be a literal or a list, and supports placeholders such as ${currentUserId} that are replaced with information about the user making the request.

expressionCondition.description: An 'expression' condition: reach into a JSON document on the resource using a JSON path, and compare the value found there. Use this for checks that depend on the case data, like 'the funds in the form are below 5000'.

type.description: Identifies this entry as a 'container' condition — a way to hop from the resource to a related resource and apply conditions there.

resourceType.description: The related resource type the nested conditions are evaluated against. Only resource types that Valtimo knows how to reach from the parent resource can be used here — for example a task can hop to its surrounding case, and a document can hop to its case definition.

oneOf.title: Case tab
oneOf.description: A tab inside the case detail view (for example the documents tab, tasks tab, or a custom tab). Grant access here to control which tabs a role can see when opening a case.

oneOf.title: Case definition
oneOf.description: A case type — the template that describes a particular kind of case (for example 'Loan request' or 'Objection'). Grant access here to control which case types a role can see or work with.

oneOf.description: An individual widget shown on a case tab (for example a fields widget, table, or custom block). Grant access here to control which widgets a role can see inside a case.

oneOf.title: Dashboard
oneOf.description: A dashboard page with widgets and metrics. Grant access here to control which dashboards a role can open.

oneOf.description: An individual case record. Documents hold the data of a single case instance and are what users actually open, edit, and complete. Grant access here to control which cases a role can view, create, modify, assign, or delete.

oneOf.description: The schema/template that describes the structure of a case document. Grant access here to control which case definitions a role can use, for example when starting a new case.

oneOf.title: Search field
oneOf.description: A configurable search field shown above a case list, used to filter cases. Grant access here to control which search fields a role can see and use.

oneOf.title: Document snapshot
oneOf.description: A historical, point-in-time copy of a case document, recorded each time it changes. Grant access here to control which roles can browse the audit history of a case.

oneOf.title: ZGW document
oneOf.description: A document stored in an external Documenten API (the Dutch ZGW document store), typically linked to a case. Grant access here to control who can read, upload, or modify these documents.

oneOf.title: IKO view
oneOf.description: An IKO (Informatie Knooppunt Omgevingswet) view — a saved query into the IKO information hub. Grant access here to control which IKO views a role can open.

oneOf.title: Case note
oneOf.description: A free-text note attached to a case, used for collaboration between case workers. Grant access here to control who can read, create, edit, or delete notes.

oneOf.title: Object (Objecten API)
oneOf.description: A record stored in an external Objecten API. Grant access here to control which objects a role can read or modify via the Objecten API integration.

oneOf.title: Uploaded resource
oneOf.description: An uploaded file or attachment stored by Valtimo. Grant access here to control who can view or download these files.

oneOf.title: Team
oneOf.description: A team of users that can be assigned to cases or tasks. Grant access here to control which teams a role can see or be assigned to.

oneOf.title: User
oneOf.description: A user account known to Valtimo (typically synchronised from Keycloak). Grant access here to control which users a role can see, for example in assignee pickers.

oneOf.title: Process instance
oneOf.description: A running process — one specific execution of a BPMN process definition. Grant access here to control who can start or interact with a process for a case.

oneOf.title: Process identity link
oneOf.description: The link between a process task and a candidate user or candidate group. Typically used together with task permissions to honour the BPMN 'Candidate groups' configuration.

oneOf.title: Process definition
oneOf.description: A BPMN process model — the template that describes a workflow. Grant access here to control which processes a role can start or view.

oneOf.title: User task
oneOf.description: A task that a user has to complete inside a running process. Grant access here to control which tasks a role can see, claim, assign, or complete.

conditions.description: Conditions applied to the related (container) resource. All conditions in the list must be true (AND) for this container condition to pass.

containerCondition.description: A 'container' condition: hop from this resource to a related resource and evaluate more conditions there. Use this for checks that involve another part of the case, like 'the surrounding case is of type loans' or 'the task belongs to a candidate group I am part of'.
